                                                                          Attachment 1

                           Initial Bargaining Proposal For
                          The San Juan Teachers Association
                            Successor Contract (2025-26)
               Amendments & Final Approval: Rep Council on April 23, 2025

Intent: The members of the San Juan Teachers’ Association offer this proposal with the
purpose of providing students with an optimal learning experience, demonstrating our
commitment to social justice and equity, and honoring the professionals that are charged
with delivering that experience.

While many articles do not require substantive changes, some articles do require clean-up
language. In some cases these are minor typographical errors and in other cases they
represents an effort to clarify the original intent of the language when it was negotiated.
These “non-substantive” changes are considered part of the proposal for this round of
bargaining, even if they are not articulated in the individual sections of this document.

Article 1 Contract Provisions
  ● Term — One year

Article 2  Grievance
   ● No substantive changes

Article 3   System of Professional Growth
   ● Revise existing language based on feedback
   ● Bring into alignment with the new CSTPs
   ● Find ways to further streamline SPG and Induction for new practitioners
   ● Explore ways to increase the number and distribution of Peer Facilitators
   ● Examine current timelines to ensure they line up with Ed Code
   ● Explore efforts to have members of ECE as Peer Facilitators
   ● Explore ways to ensure the participation of administrative facilitators in the SPG
       process
   ●   Explore ways to have temporary and probationary practitioners receive peer
       facilitator support

Article 4      Transfers
   ● Explore options for increased flexibility in the voluntary transfer process
   ● Examine timelines in light of efforts to recruit new practitioners to our system
   ● Explore ways to improve the transfer rights for ‘district programs’ such as elementary
       specialists, special ed service providers, etc.

Article 5   Leaves
   ● Continue to improve leaves, specifically: sick leave and Personal Necessity (PN)
          ○ Clarifying language regarding sick leave / PN partial day (15 minute increments)
          ○ Examine restrictions on use of PN time
          ○ Examine options to increase the amount of PN / sick leave
          ○ Clarify that both mental health and physical health exist under sick leave

                                                                                               1
                                                                                                   725
         ○​ Explore ways to rename leave days (no designation)
   ●​ Explore leaves to “Bereavement Leave”

Article 6 ​ Member Safety
   ●​ Continue to improve safety and security on campus
   ●​ Secure needed supplies for speech and language pathologists, nurses, social workers,
       counselors, academic intervention specialists, elementary specialists, and others
   ●​ Secure appropriate space for speech and language pathologists, nurses, social workers,
       counselors, academic intervention specialists, elementary specialists, and others
   ●​ Explore options for clearly marked staff parking
   ●​ Explore options for increasing classroom and school based safety measures, such as
       curtain maintenance in every classroom and fencing
   ●​ Explore ways to ensure practitioner voice in modifications to school sites
   ●​ Explore ways to support practitioners when dealing with threatening and/or aggressive
       behavior on campus (both student and adult behaviors)
   ●​ Explore create safety and supports for practitioners who follow CA state law (FAIR
       Act)
Article 7 ​ Class Size
  ●​ Examine ratios and caseload maximums for speech and language pathologists, nurses,
      counselors, adapted PE Specialists, vision and mobility specialists, elementary
      specialists, and others
          ○​ Explore options for increasing the individualized attention to each student
              deserves
  ●​ Explore the creation of ratios or caseload maximums for social workers and academic
      intervention specialists
  ●​ Explore options to reduce PE ratios
  ●​ Address the workload challenges of special education service providers in relation to
      class size, caseload, designation of assignment and IEPs
          ○​ Explore ways to support members when they are at caseload and class size
              maximums and having pending assessments
          ○​ Address challenges in the Blended Preschool model including loss of preparation
              time, class size, and class makeup
          ○​ Explore ways to compensate practitioners when prep and/or lunch is lost when
              supporting students
  ●​ Explore ways to enhance and expand the work of the Class Size Reduction (CSR) MOU
          ○​ Explore opportunities to reduce ratios (elementary) and class size reduction
              related to ELD students
          ○​ Explore opportunities to expand the implementation of CSR at the current six
              sites
          ○​ Explore opportunities to identify additional school sites that meet the criteria of
              high density and ability to implement CSR
  ●​ Explore opportunities to provide additional ongoing support positions or to fund and
      maintain current support positions where such positions have shown to be helpful.
  ●​ Examine possible supports for general education teachers to more fully support students
      with IEPs being mainstreamed
  ●​ Explore ways to continue intervention and IA positions at elementary schools


                                                                                               2
                                                                                                   726
   ●​ Explore ways to bring primary (K-3) elementary grades to an average of 26
   ●​ Explore ways to reduce class size for combination classes
   ●​ Explore ways to increase IA support for Special Education Teachers
   ●​ Explore options to reduce math and English at middle school levels

Article 8   ​ Hours

   ●​ Address the workload (e.g. paper work, assessments, IEP time and other required
      educational meetings) and working conditions for all specialized groups including
      special education teachers, nurses, counselors, speech and language pathologists,
      academic instructional specialists, social workers, vision and mobility specialists, teacher
      librarians, and others
   ●​ Establish an agreement around the 2026-27 and 2027-28 work year calendars
   ●​ Explore ways to minimize use of elementary specialists for sub coverage
   ●​ Examine the Emergency Backup Practitioner SLA for potential incorporation of elements
      into the Collective Bargaining Agreement
   ●​ Explore expanding the number of Preparation Thursdays
   ●​ Consider options to provide more flexibility with minimum days.
   ●​ Establish language that acknowledges that all practitioners have preparation time, not just
      classroom teachers
   ●​ Explore ways to standardized NID length and reduce impact on practitioners
   ●​ Explore ways to increase compensation for missed prep in Elementary
   ●​ Explore ways to increase number of and increase access to, Equity days for Special
      Education providers

Article 9 ​ Employee Benefits
   ●​ Continue to provide quality cost-effective health care coverage in all areas
   ●​ Explore options to increase dependent coverage (including Vision and Dental)
   ●​ Explore additional options for dental coverage
   ●​ Explore options to increase compensation for early notification of retirement
   ●​ Explore ways to ensure members have access to the medications and medical
      equipment (pharmaceutical packages)
   ●​ Explore ways to add holistic and natural types of treatment and medicines

Article 10 ​ Pre-retirement Work Program
   ●​ Explore ways to strengthen notification to members

Article 11 ​ Salary
   ●​ Explore opportunities for a fair increase to all parts of the salary schedule (beginning,
       middle, & end) to competitively recruit and retain and all bargaining unit members
           ○​ Explore options to enhance all stipend schedules
           ○​ Explore opportunities to create new stipends
           ○​ Explore stipends to those that hold child development permits
   ●​ Explore the opportunity to contractualize language related to members having the option
       to be paid 12 times per year.
           ○​ Examine how to ensure that this option is available to all members


                                                                                                  3
                                                                                                      727
   ●​ Explore ways to clarify Intern Salary placement
   ●​ Explore counting all years of creditable service for placement on the salary schedule
   ●​ Examine the option to allow Interns to move to the TK12 salary schedule mid-year, once
      they complete their credential
   ●​ Explore ways to expand the Hard to Staff position list
   ●​ Explore ways to compensate members when accurate pay is delayed

Article 12 ​ Professional Dues and Payroll Programs
   ●​ No substantive changes

Article 13 ​ Definitions
  ●​ No substantive changes

Article 14 ​ Adult Education
  ●​ Provide fair cost of living adjustments and improve salary and stipend schedules that
       competitively recruit, retain, and reward all bargaining unit members

Article 15 ​ (New) Schools of Innovation
  ●​ Establish and/or expand ways to support schools that engage in an alternative educational
       approach (Montessori, IB, etc.) ​

Article 16 ​ Just Cause, Due Process, and Progressive Discipline
   ●​ No substantive changes

Article 17 ​ Association Rights
  ●​ No substantive changes
  ●​ Explore ways to recognize service of faculty reps, not located at secondary schools
  ●​ Explore how to bring Exhibit E (Notice of All Bargaining Unit Members) into alignment
       with current law

Article 18 ​ Probationary Teachers
   ●​ Explore options to eliminate / reduce the impact of bias related to the retention of
       probationary teachers

Article 19 ​ Professional Growth For Credential Renewal— Abolished by legislative
             action in 2006
  ●​ No Change

Article 20 ​ Summer School
   ●​ Explore ways to increase summer school pay
   ●​ Explore ways to increase pay for ESY (extended school year)
   ●​ Explore ways to reduce the impact on PE/Health FTE from students taking PE/Health in
       summer school

Article 21 ​ Early Childhood Education
   ●​ Provide fair cost of living adjustments and improve salary and stipend schedules that
       competitively recruit, retain and reward all bargaining unit members


                                                                                              4
                                                                                                  728
          ○​ Explore ways to compensate ECE practitioners who hold a site supervisor permit,
              but have not yet earned a BA
   ●​ Clean up language that is outdated
   ●​ Address the workload challenges of ECE teachers
   ●​ Explore ways to address sub shortage in ECE
   ●​ Explore ways to support Discovery Club teachers with students with more intensive
      needs ( toileting, behavior, etc.)
   ●​ Establish class size maximums for Discovery Club and adult/student ratios
   ●​ Explore ways to support all ECE teachers who work with students who display
      aggressive behaviors and/or other intensive needs
   ●​ Explore ways to support ECE teachers in gaining certification and or education who want
      to teach TK
   ●​ Explore ways to compensate teachers with directors permits and credentials
   ●​ Explore ways to ensure that Discovery Club teacher receive prep time
   ●​ Explore ways to improve the quality of ECE subs
   ●​ Explore ways to improve physical safety
   ●​ Explore ways to allow Content Specialists to reapply (without returning to the
      classroom)
   ●​ Explore how to move ECE TOSAs and Content Specialists from article 25 to article
      21 (job description does not match)

Article 22 ​ Independent Study / Alternative Education
   ●​ Provide fair cost of living adjustments and improve salary and stipend schedules that
       competitively recruit, retain, and reward all bargaining unit members
   ●​ Incorporate all MOUs and SLAs into the contract

Article 23 ​ Home and Hospital
   ●​ Provide fair cost of living adjustments and improve salary and stipend schedules that
       competitively recruit, retain, and reward all bargaining unit members
   ●​ Explore ways to allow HHI teachers to pick up additional FTE assignments (HHI
       teachers start at 60%)

Article 24 ​ Creating and Sustaining A Collaborative Culture
   ●​ No substantive changes

Article 25 ​ Teachers On Special Assignment
   ●​ No substantive changes

Article 26 ​ Teacher Interns
   ●​ Explore options regarding internships for practitioners to increase the diversity of the
       profession and address practitioner shortage(s)
   ●​ Improve district supports for interns

                               Timeline

●​ December 2024 / January 2025: Drafting of Bargaining Survey -
   Approved by Executive Board and Bargaining Team
●​ February: Bargaining Survey Provided to members (996 Responses)
●​ March: Executive Board and Bargaining Team review Bargaining
   Survey results
       ○​ Use results to craft Draft Bargaining proposal
●​ March Rep Council: Draft of Initial Bargaining Proposal presented to
   Rep Council members
●​ March 19 – April 23: Faculty Reps lead discussions at their sites for
   feedback and revisions
●​ April Rep Council: Amendments and final approval of Initial
   Bargaining by Rep Council
●​ April 24: Initial Proposal “sunshined” to District for presentation at
   first School Board meeting in May
